description: Per-query enforcement bypass patterns from paper-arxiv.md §3.3. Each pattern is a deterministic SQL generator producing N variants that, individually, lack a syntactic anomaly the per-query layer can anchor on. Session-aware enforcement is expected to catch them via cumulative state. Column pools match the schema in deploy/postgres/init.sql.

patterns.name: Missing LIMIT on data table
patterns.rationale: SELECT with specific columns and WHERE clause but no LIMIT. Per-query: ALLOW (no anomaly). Session: DENY after threshold of denied_tables hits.
patterns.template: SELECT {cols} FROM {table} WHERE tenant_id = 'tenant_1' AND created_at > '2024-01-01'

patterns.name: ORDER BY RANDOM() probing
patterns.rationale: Repeated random-ordered samples of a table. Each query is below the per-query deny threshold (risk_score = 1). Session: DENY after threshold of similar queries.
patterns.template: SELECT id, {col} FROM {table} WHERE tenant_id = 'tenant_1' ORDER BY RANDOM() LIMIT 50

patterns.name: LIMIT/OFFSET stepping
patterns.rationale: Sequential pagination through a table with monotonic OFFSET. Each query individually plausible. Session: DENY at threshold via CatSessionBlock.
patterns.template: SELECT id, {col} FROM {table} WHERE tenant_id = 'tenant_1' ORDER BY id LIMIT 100 OFFSET {offset}
